degraded.detail: F0 ran the two affected plugin suites (shipwright-grade 352 passed + shipwright-compliance 958 passed) + ruff@0.15.15 (changed dirs) + leak-guard f0 ALLOW. The change is isolated to the Control-Grade engine (compliance lib) + the grade cold projector/docs; grep confirmed no compute_grade/GradeInputs consumers in shared/, integration-tests/, or scripts/. The full shared/tests + all-plugin + integration suite runs in CI on the PR.

degraded.condition: network empirical fixtures not re-recorded (by design)
degraded.detail: The 7 committed network fixtures were NOT re-recorded. This is intentional: change_traceability_measurable defaults True, so the stored GradeInputs replay via GradeInputs(**gi) with the dimension still scored -> network grades byte-identical. The offline calibration suite (test_run_empirical + calibration.grade_from_fixture) is green; the network -m empirical suite (opt-in, needs gh) was not run locally.

surface_verification.justification: The change is to the deterministic Control-Grade Python engine (compliance lib) + the shipwright-grade cold projector + report copy/docs. There is no web/UI/API/server surface and no served client; the grade HTML report is a static artifact covered by the golden-snapshot test (regenerated, copy-only diff). The CLI grader IS the executable surface and was driven END-TO-END as the behavior proof: (1) the shitstorm case -- a synthetic well-run repo with clean Conventional Commits but no #N refs -- graded F 49.0 'out of control' on HEAD and B 89.0 with change_traceability n/a after the fix; (2) the dogfood monorepo graded A 99.9 authoritative with change_traceability measured (276/276). Verification = F0 pytest (1310) + ruff + leak-guard f0 ALLOW + 2 real-CLI end-to-end grades.
